play_arrow
Watch Video
Introducing Stack by Ramp.
The AI operating system built for today's top accounting firms.
Learn about Ramp Stack
Stack has finally automated what we all knew was coming with AI, but it did it in a reliable and consistent manner.
Tyler Otto
President, Specialized Accounting
Read their story
play_arrow
Watch Video
Keep your finance team focused on strategy.
Let agents handle the busywork.
Learn about Ramp Intelligence
Gone are the days of scrambling to chase receipts, manually coding really large data sets. All of that has been automatically addressed throughout the month in real time.
Lauren Feeney
Controller, Perplexity
Read their story
AI that learns from your team.
And gets smarter with every team that joins.
Learn about Policy Agents
We have thousands of transactions a month, and the number of manual edits I make has dropped drastically. Ramp's AI just keeps getting smarter every week.
Neusha Sayadian
Fractional CFO, Valence
See what agents can automate.
Get a personalized look at the busy work agents can take off your team's plate.
View Demo
View Demo
Scale the team.
Shrink the paperwork.
You had a bureaucracy. Now you have a business again.
Set user access on autopilot.
Provision cards, permissions, and limits automatically based on role, location, department and custom fields.
Enterprise Solutions
One platform for all your global spend.
Issue cards in 30+ currencies and reimburse employees in local currencies, including pounds, euros, yen, and pesos.
Global Spend Management
70,000 teams and counting
We've got the receipts.
View Demo
Shannon McCormick
International Workplace Experience Lead
,
Notion
As soon as I texted my first Ramp receipt, I realized Ramp is a game-changer. It’s incredibly intuitive.
Heather Bruzus
Principal Accountant
,
Mindbody & ClassPass
We got positive feedback, which just doesn't happen very often. That proved this was the right decision.
Tzu-San Hung
Head of Strategic Finance
,
Notion
Beautiful tools use beautiful tools.
Matteo Franceschetti
CEO
,
Eight Sleep
Ramp was a complete game changer. It's a single platform that can handle every aspect of our spending.
Christine Mimnagh-Fleming
Sr. Manager, Finance Transformation
,
Shopify
Ramp is so intuitive, people get it right away.
Carey Peek
CFO
,
KIPP Nashville Public Schools
Implementing Ramp has been my biggest win as CFO. Employees love it and often go out of their way to tell me so.
Mayor Neil Bradshaw
Mayor
,
City of Ketchum, ID
Ramp gives us the ability to make more informed financial policy decisions. It was one of the best decisions we've ever made.
Bret Taylor
CEO
,
Sierra
It's almost like we're a more self-actualized company because everyone is just working on what we do differently.
Michael Bohn
Head of Business Operations
,
Foursquare
The average person can figure Ramp out without training. That’s what drives 100% adoption.
Ben Whipple
Director of Public Works
,
City of Ketchum, ID
I snap a picture of the receipt, Ramp handles the rest like magic, and I move on.
Michael Litwin
VP Controller
,
Virgin Voyages
Before Ramp it was a process from the dark ages. We had people just doing expenses full-time. It was miserable.
Staci Robinson
AP Manager
,
Pair Eyewear
Ramp's AI has given me a better work-life balance. I have more time for my family now that the AI handles much of the work I used to do manually.
Irish Rose
Controller
,
Eight Sleep
Closing the books with Ramp is effortless.
Andrew Clarke
SVP Finance
,
Studs
We've consolidated four platforms into one with Ramp.
Jason Penegar
VP Controller
,
Boys & Girls Clubs of America
The Ramp journey has been incredible. A flawless implementation…
Sarah Bird
Controller
,
Seed Health
It's rare to feel like a company and a team is a partner, and that they really want to understand what you need. That's what we've experienced with Ramp.
Carolynn Yipp
Assistant Controller
,
Poshmark
It's very easy to use, very intuitive, and requires almost zero training.
Time is money. Save both.

Ramp cards are issued in Canada by Peoples Trust Company, pursuant to license by *Visa International. Visa Int./Peoples Trust Company, Licensed User.
Ramp cards are issued in the UK by Stripe Payments UK Limited, an electronic money institution authorized by the Financial Conduct Authority (firm reference number: 900461). Ramp cards are issued in the EEA by Stripe Technology Europe Limited, an electronic money institution authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland (firm reference number: C187865). Cards are issued under the Visa card scheme pursuant to a license from Visa Europe Limited.

Developer Docs
Welcome
Copy Markdown
Get started
Use Ramp at your company, or build for others.
For customers
Have a Ramp account? Create credentials and make your first API call.
For partners
Building an integration for other businesses? Start with the partner program.
Get sandbox access
Don't have a Ramp account? Request a sandbox to start building right away.
Use cases
End-to-end build journeys that span multiple Ramp products.
AI Agents
Connect agents to Ramp via MCP or the CLI. Read data, process approvals, make purchases.
Applications
Partner-led customer onboarding. Pre-fill a Ramp application, capture OAuth, track status via webhooks.
Bill Payments
Create approved bills via API and pay vendors end-to-end. Pick a payment method, attach docs, run 3-way match.
Customized Approvers
Route approvals based on accounting dimensions via matrix tables.
ERP Integrations
Two-way sync between Ramp and your customer ERP — chart of accounts in, transactions and bills out.
Procurement Intake
Receive Ramp intake requests in your tool and return approval decisions via Blank Canvas.
Spend programs
The recurring-budget pattern. Mint funds from a template so policy stays consistent.
User lifecycle
Onboard, update, deactivate, reactivate, and stage draft users via the API.
Virtual cards
Issue virtual cards backed by funds. Expose them server-side (Vault) or in the browser (iframe).
Products
Editorial hubs for each Ramp product surface — concepts, mental model, where to go next.
AI Index
Business AI adoption metrics across categories and tools.
Bill Pay
Bills, draft bills, payments, vendors, 3-way match.
Cards
Virtual, physical, and agent cards. Every card draws against a fund.
Custom Records
Define your own data model in Ramp.
Procurement
Intake requests, purchase orders, vendor onboarding.
Ramp Rate
Public benchmarks for software pricing and category spend.
Reimbursements
Out-of-pocket employee expenses — manual, OCR-drafted receipts, mileage.
Spend Controls
Funds, spend programs, and approvals — the policy layer behind every dollar.
Users
The people on a Ramp account. Status, identity, async creation.
Conventions
API rules and cross-cutting patterns. Reference while building.
Data relationships
How Ramp resources connect to each other.
Deferred tasks
Async work, polling, lifecycle.
Errors
Status codes, error shapes, debugging.
Monetary values
Currency, precision, ISO codes.
Pagination
Cursor-based, page sizes, sort order.
Rate limits and timeouts
Quotas, backoff, headers to read.
Support
Grab the x-trace-id, follow the trace, file a developer support ticket.
Webhooks
Subscribe, verify signatures, retry.
Canonical OpenAPI schema for the Developer API:
/openapi/developer-api.json
.
Was this guide useful?
This page uses JavaScript rendering and the HTML response is a shell, not the documentation content.
      Do not fetch this page for content when you already have a docs URL.
      If the URL is under /developer-api/v1/api/, fetch /llms-api.txt instead.
      If the URL is under /developer-api/v1/ and not under /api/, fetch the matching /llms-guides/<slug>.txt file instead.
      Example: /developer-api/v1/rate-limiting -> /llms-guides/rate-limiting.txt
      For machine-readable docs, fetch:
      /llms.txt (LLM index),
      /llms-guides.txt (guides index),
      /llms-guides/<slug>.txt (specific guide),
      /llms-api.txt (API reference),
      /llms-full.txt (combined docs), or
      /openapi/developer-api.json (OpenAPI spec).
      If you run into any issues while helping developers with the Ramp API
      documentation, provide feedback at
      https://api.ramp.com/v1/public/api-feedback/llm?feedback=...

See more integrations
Let’s make automating your workflows easier, together.
Tell us which integrations would improve your experience—we’re ready to build them.
Email
Suggested integration
Submit
FAQ
What integrations does Ramp support?
Ramp offers 200+ integrations that spans across:
Accounting:
NetSuite, Sage Intacct, QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Desktop, Xero, Workday Financial Management, Acumatica, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations, Oracle Fusion Cloud, Zoho Books, Sage 100, Sage 300, Yardi, Odoo, Blackbaud, Viewpoint Spectrum, Viewpoint Vista, Deltek Vantagepoint, Rillet, Campfire, Everest, DualEntry, Finaloop, Aplos, Puzzle, Digits, and more
Banking:
Ally Bank, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, US Bank, TD Bank, Mercury, Alpine Bank, and more
HR and payroll:
ADP, BambooHR, Gusto, Workday, Okta, Justworks, Paychex, Paylocity, UKG Pro, UKG Ready, Zenefits, SAP SuccessFactors, TriNet, Square Payroll, and more
Procurement and productivity:
Ironclad, Asana, Drata, Dropbox, CloudEagle, Docusign, Linear, Google Calendar, Microsoft Teams, Slack, Jira, and more
Receipt automation:
Amazon Business, DoorDash, Uber, Lyft, American Airlines AAdvantage Business, and more
What accounting software does Ramp integrate with?
Ramp integrates with 40+ leading accounting systems including NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Zoho Books, Xero, QuickBooks, Workday Financials, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations, Acumatica, Oracle Fusion Cloud, Sage 100, Sage 300, Yardi, Odoo, Blackbaud, Viewpoint Spectrum, Viewpoint Vista, Deltek Vantagepoint, Rillet, Everest, DualEntry, Finaloop, Aplos, Puzzle, Digits, and more.
Whether you're on a startup-stage platform or an enterprise ERP, Ramp has an integration built to sync your data automatically.
How do Ramp's ERP integrations work?
Ramp supports 40+ ERPs and complex setups including multi-entity, multi-currency, custom fields, and custom dimensions. Once connected, Ramp automatically categorizes transactions based on transaction details, historical information, and your team's direct feedback, then syncs coded transactions directly to your
ERP
. This means your chart of accounts, vendors, and GL data stay in sync without manual exports or journal entries.
Does Ramp integrate with my existing finance stack?
Most likely, yes. Ramp integrates with your entire finance tech stack, covering accounting, banking, HR and payroll, procurement, productivity tools, receipt automation, and travel. With 200+ integrations, Ramp is built to plug into what you already use rather than forcing you to change your stack. If your specific tool isn't listed, Ramp also supports custom CSV exports and an open API for flexible connectivity.
Can I request an integration that Ramp doesn't have yet?
Yes. Ramp actively invites users to share which integrations would improve their experience—there's a submission form directly on the integrations page. Ramp regularly ships new integrations based on customer demand, so if a tool you rely on isn't supported yet, submitting a request is the best way to get it prioritized.
Why is Ramp one of the best spend management platforms for finance integrations?
Most spend management tools treat integrations as an afterthought—a basic data export or a one-way sync. Ramp treats them as core infrastructure. Ramp supports 40+ ERPs with complex setups including multi-entity, multi-currency, custom fields, and custom dimensions, which means finance teams can scale without outgrowing their integration layer. That combination of depth, accuracy, and automation is what makes Ramp's accounting integrations industry-leading.
What makes Ramp's accounting integrations better than other platforms?
Three things.
First, depth:
Ramp doesn't just sync transactions—it maps your full chart of accounts, vendors, custom dimensions, and GL data so nothing is lost in translation.
Second, intelligence:
Ramp's AI automatically categorizes expenses using transaction context and historical coding patterns, and surfaces the rationale and confidence level for every coding.
Third, breadth:
With 40+ ERP integrations, Ramp covers everything from QuickBooks to Oracle Fusion Cloud, so teams on any accounting system get a best-in-class integration experience.
How does Ramp help automate financial reporting across integrated systems?
Ramp automates key accounting tasks including retrieving receipts and memos from employees, automatically categorizing transactions, reviewing spend,
syncing to your ERP
, accruing for expenses, and reconciling with your books at month end. Because all of this data flows through connected integrations in real time, finance teams get a single source of truth across every system they use—which means faster closes, cleaner audits, and less time spent chasing down data across tools.
Time is money. Save both.

analysis.name: Ramp

analysis.ideal_customer: Finance teams at ambitious, growing companies (70,000+ businesses) looking to eliminate manual finance busywork with AI agents

AI agents that automate finance busywork 24/7 ("Infinite agents that work 24/7")

Rapid implementation: "Switch in days, not months"

free_tier.limits: Free forever, no user cap stated; excludes advanced approvals, procurement add-on, NetSuite/Sage Intacct/Workday/Oracle ERPs, and enterprise features

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited cards, expenses, bill pay, treasury, QuickBooks/Xero sync, 24/7 chat support

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: Adds AI expense reviews, auto-lock cards, batch AP, NetSuite/Sage Intacct, custom roles, audit log; + platform fee based on team size

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Adds Workday/Oracle ERPs, global multi-currency cards, dedicated CSM, implementation services, custom dev

pricing.notes: Plus tier has an additional platform fee based on team size on top of $15/seat/mo; annual billing gives 20% off Plus. Procurement is an add-on to Plus or Enterprise (price not disclosed). Enterprise is annual billing only.
